docs.text: x86decomp llm prompt ./jobs/func-401000.json ./prompts/func-401000.json

docs.title: llm generate
docs.text:
Generate one uncompiled C proposal.

x86decomp llm generate PROFILE JOB OUTPUT \
  [--report PATH] [--overwrite]
Argument Required Description PROFILE yes Path to the LLM profile JSON JOB yes Path to the job JSON file OUTPUT yes Path for the generated C source file --report no Path for the generation report --overwrite no Overwrite existing output file

The output is an untrusted proposal. The report claim is proposal_only.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp llm generate ./config/llm/codellama.json ./jobs/func-401000.json \
  ./candidates/func-401000.c --report ./reports/gen-401000.json

docs.title: llm cpp-generate
docs.text:
Generate one bounded C++ proposal using a local-model profile.

x86decomp llm cpp-generate PROFILE JOB OUTPUT \
  [--class-context PATH] [--report PATH] [--overwrite]
Argument Required Description PROFILE yes Path to the LLM profile JSON JOB yes Path to the job JSON file OUTPUT yes Path for the generated C++ source file --class-context no Path to a class-context JSON file --report no Path for the generation report --overwrite no Overwrite existing output file

Proposal-only

cpp-generate is proposal-only. It does not change the v0.7.5 exact-byte acceptance boundary. C++ proposals do not participate in the closed exact-match loop.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp llm cpp-generate ./config/llm/codellama.json ./jobs/func-401000.json \
  ./candidates/func-401000.cpp \
  --class-context ./context/classes.json \
  --report ./reports/cpp-gen-401000.json

docs.title: llm match
docs.text:
Run bounded generation, compilation, relocation resolution, and exact byte matching.

x86decomp llm match PROFILE COMPILER_PROFILE JOB OUTPUT_DIRECTORY \
  [--max-attempts N] [--overwrite]
Argument Required Description PROFILE yes Path to the LLM profile JSON COMPILER_PROFILE yes Path to the compiler profile JSON JOB yes Path to the job JSON file OUTPUT_DIRECTORY yes Output directory for all loop artifacts --max-attempts no Override the job's max attempts --overwrite no Overwrite existing output directory

The loop stops when exact byte identity is established, the model returns a valid blocked response, or the attempt limit is exhausted. Each attempt preserves the prompt, model response, candidate C, compiler report, COFF symbol metadata, relocation resolution report, and exact byte-comparison report.

docs.title: Acceptance gate
docs.text:
A candidate reaches byte_matched only when all of:

  1. Response is exactly one valid JSON object with required keys
  2. Proposed source passes the C-source contract
  3. Compiler profile completes successfully
  4. Output is a COFF object for the declared architecture
  5. Requested symbol exists with one extractable function body
  6. Every COFF relocation resolves from explicit job inputs
  7. Resolved candidate length equals target length
  8. Every resolved candidate byte equals the target byte

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp llm match ./config/llm/codellama.json \
  ./config/compiler-profiles/msvc-2019-x86.json \
  ./jobs/func-401000.json \
  ./match-output/func-401000 \
  --max-attempts 8

docs.title: llm batch-match
docs.text:
Run bounded match loops for a deterministic job queue.

x86decomp llm batch-match PROFILE COMPILER_PROFILE JOBS OUTPUT_DIRECTORY \
  [--max-workers 1] [--max-attempts N] [--overwrite]
Argument Required Description PROFILE yes Path to the LLM profile JSON COMPILER_PROFILE yes Path to the compiler profile JSON JOBS yes Path to the job queue directory OUTPUT_DIRECTORY yes Output directory for match results --max-workers no Maximum concurrent workers (default: 1) --max-attempts no Override per-job max attempts --overwrite no Overwrite existing output

Single-worker deterministic

v0.7.11 batch-match is deterministic and single-worker. Pass --max-workers 1.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp llm batch-match ./config/llm/codellama.json \
  ./config/compiler-profiles/msvc-2019-x86.json \
  ./batch-jobs \
  ./batch-output \
  --max-attempts 8

docs.title: llm verify
docs.text:
Verify a local-model byte-match report and accepted artifact hashes.

x86decomp llm verify REPORT
Argument Required Description REPORT yes Path to the report.json from a match-loop output directory

Verifies status and attempt invariants, path containment, accepted source/object/resolved-byte hashes, zero unresolved relocations, and equality between the accepted resolved-byte hash and target hash.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp llm verify ./match-output/func-401000/report.json

docs.title: Security boundary
docs.text:
• Profiles are loopback-only by default; use --allow-remote to override
  • API keys via environment variable name only; never embedded in profiles
  • Same-origin-only redirects; bounded request timeouts and response sizes
  • TLS verification enabled by default
  • No native target execution; no direct workflow-state mutation
  • Strict source rejection for inline assembly, byte emission, naked functions

Untrusted proposals

LLM output is an untrusted proposal at every stage. The byte_matched status is granted exclusively by deterministic gates (compiler, relocation resolution, byte comparison). Relocation-normalized similarity, instruction similarity, finite dynamic equivalence, and model confidence do not satisfy this gate.

docs.title: Analysis: COFF Commands
docs.text: Commands for inspecting, extracting, synthesizing, and resolving COFF (Common Object File Format) objects and static libraries.

docs.title: x86decomp coff-inspect
docs.text: Inspects COFF object metadata, symbols, and relocations.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp coff-inspect OBJECT

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses a COFF object file and returns its full structure: file header (machine type, section count, timestamp, characteristics), section headers, symbol table, and relocation entries. This is a read-only, non-mutating static analysis operation.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description OBJECT Yes path COFF object file (.obj) to inspect.

docs.title: Return fields
docs.text: Field Description machine Target machine type (e.g., 0x014C for x86). architecture "x86" or "x86_64". section_count Number of sections. symbol_count Number of symbols in the symbol table. symbols Per-symbol name, value, section number, type, storage class, auxiliary records. sections Per-section name, size, raw data offset, relocation count, characteristics, COMDAT info. relocations Per-section relocation entries: virtual address, symbol table index, type.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp coff-inspect function.obj

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• lib-inspect — inspect static/import libraries
  • coff-extract — extract a symbol payload

docs.title: x86decomp lib-inspect
docs.text: Inspects a COFF archive (static library or import library).

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp lib-inspect LIBRARY

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses a COFF archive file (.lib) and returns its member list, linker symbol table, and per-member metadata. This is a read-only, non-mutating static analysis operation.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description LIBRARY Yes path COFF archive file (.lib) to inspect.

docs.title: Return fields
docs.text: Field Description member_count Total number of archive members. linker_symbols Number of symbols in the archive symbol table. members Per-member name, kind (coff_object or import_object), size, and offset.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp lib-inspect libc.lib

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• coff-inspect — inspect individual COFF objects
  • coff-comdat-resolve — resolve COMDAT groups

docs.title: x86decomp coff-extract
docs.text: Extracts one symbol's payload bytes from a COFF object.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp coff-extract OBJECT SYMBOL OUTPUT [--size SIZE]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses the COFF object, locates the named symbol, and writes its raw data bytes to OUTPUT. Optionally truncates or validates against an expected --size.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description OBJECT Yes path COFF object file to extract from. SYMBOL Yes string Symbol name to locate in the symbol table. OUTPUT Yes path Destination file for raw symbol data bytes. Parent directories are created automatically.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --size (none) Expected symbol size in bytes (base-prefixed). When provided, the extracted payload is truncated or validated to this size.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp coff-extract function.obj _myFunction raw_bytes.bin --size 0x200

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• coff-inspect — find symbol names and sizes first
  • diff-function — compare extracted symbol to a PE function

docs.title: x86decomp coff-synthesize
docs.text: Creates a synthetic COFF object from raw machine code and relocation records.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp coff-synthesize CODE SYMBOL OUTPUT --architecture {x86,x86_64} [--relocations RELOCATIONS]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Reads raw machine-code bytes from CODE, wraps them as a COFF object with the given symbol name, and writes the complete COFF to OUTPUT. Optionally applies relocation entries from a JSON file. The output machine type is 0x014C for x86 and 0x8664 for x86-64.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description CODE Yes path File containing raw machine-code bytes for the .text section. SYMBOL Yes string Symbol name for the synthesized function. OUTPUT Yes path Destination path for the generated COFF object.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --architecture x86 x86 or x86_64. Determines the COFF machine type. --relocations (none) JSON file containing relocation entries. Each entry must have offset (int), type (int), and optionally symbol_index (int, default 0) and symbol_name (string).

docs.title: Relocation entry schema
docs.text:
[
  {
    "offset": 1,
    "type": 20,
    "symbol_index": 0,
    "symbol_name": "__imp__ExitProcess@4"
  }
]

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp coff-synthesize code.bin _myFunc synth.obj --architecture x86 --relocations relocs.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• coff-inspect — verify the synthesized object
  • coff-extract — extract a symbol from a real COFF object

docs.title: x86decomp coff-comdat-resolve
docs.text: Resolves COMDAT groups across multiple COFF objects.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp coff-comdat-resolve OBJECTS... [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses all supplied COFF objects, identifies COMDAT sections (sections with the IMAGE_SCN_LNK_COMDAT flag), and resolves duplicate COMDAT groups by selecting one definition per group. This is useful for understanding which objects contribute which COMDAT sections in a link.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description OBJECTS... Yes (1+) paths One or more COFF object files to analyze.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report (none) If provided, writes the resolved COMDAT result as JSON to this path in addition to stdout.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp coff-comdat-resolve obj1.obj obj2.obj obj3.obj --report comdat_report.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• coff-inspect — inspect individual objects for COMDAT sections
  • linker-plan — build a linker reconstruction plan using COFF objects

docs.title: x86decomp disassemble
docs.text: Decodes bounded machine-code instructions using Capstone.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp disassemble CODE --base BASE --architecture {x86,x86_64} [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Reads raw machine-code bytes from CODE, decodes every instruction using the Capstone disassembly engine, and returns a list of instruction records. Each record includes the address, mnemonic, operand string, raw bytes, and size. This is a read-only, non-mutating static analysis operation.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description CODE Yes path Binary file containing raw machine-code bytes. --base No (default 0) int (base-prefixed) Base virtual address added to each instruction offset. Supports decimal (4096), hex (0x1000), and octal (0o10000). --architecture No (default x86) x86 or x86_64 Target instruction set architecture.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report (none) If provided, writes the full disassembly result as JSON to this path in addition to stdout.

docs.title: Return value
docs.text:
{
  "instructions": [
    {
      "address": 4198400,
      "mnemonic": "push",
      "op_str": "ebp",
      "bytes": "55",
      "size": 1
    }
  ]
}

Each instruction record includes:

Field Description address base + offset virtual address. mnemonic Capstone instruction mnemonic. op_str Capstone operand string (may be empty). bytes Hex-encoded raw instruction bytes. size Instruction length in bytes.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp disassemble code.bin --base 0x401000 --architecture x86 --report disasm.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text: • crosscheck-ghidra — compare toolkit disassembly against Ghidra export

docs.title: x86decomp crosscheck-ghidra
docs.text: Compares the toolkit's Capstone-based disassembly against a Ghidra instruction export.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp crosscheck-ghidra INSTRUCTIONS_JSONL CODE --base BASE --architecture {x86,x86_64} [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Reads a Ghidra-exported JSONL file of instructions, then independently disassembles the same raw machine-code bytes with Capstone. Compares each instruction at matching addresses and reports mismatches, extra instructions, and missing instructions. This provides an independent cross-verification of the Ghidra disassembly output.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description INSTRUCTIONS_JSONL Yes path Ghidra export file in JSON lines format (one instruction per line). CODE Yes path Raw machine-code bytes corresponding to the Ghidra export range. --base Required int (base-prefixed) Base address for instruction offsets. Must match the address range in the Ghidra export. --architecture No (default x86) x86 or x86_64 Target instruction set architecture.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report (none) If provided, writes the comparison result as JSON to this path in addition to stdout.

docs.title: Return value
docs.text:
The comparison result includes match statistics and per-address mismatch details:

Field Description total_ghidra Number of instructions in the Ghidra export. total_capstone Number of instructions decoded by Capstone. matches Number of instructions with identical mnemonics and operands. mismatches Count and list of instruction-level differences. extra Instructions found by Capstone but not in Ghidra. missing Instructions in Ghidra but not decoded by Capstone.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp crosscheck-ghidra ghidra_export.jsonl code.bin --base 0x401000 --architecture x86 --report crosscheck.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• disassemble — standalone Capstone disassembly
  • ghidra-export — export instructions from Ghidra

docs.title: x86decomp metadata-scan
docs.text: Recovers bounded MSVC metadata from a PE image: RTTI, vtables, unwind info, TLS callbacks, and initializer tables.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp metadata-scan PE [--object OBJ...] [--map MAP] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Scans a PE image for MSVC-generated metadata structures using pattern matching and section analysis. Recovers:

  • RTTI: Complete object locators, type descriptors, class hierarchy descriptors, and base class arrays.
  • Vtables: Virtual function tables identified via RTTI cross-references.
  • Unwind info: Exception handler and unwind data (.pdata/.xdata) for both x86 and x86-64.
  • TLS: Thread-local storage callbacks and data directories.
  • Initializers: __xc_a/__xc_z initializer arrays and __xi_a/__xi_z C++ static initializers.

Optional COFF objects and linker map provide additional cross-reference evidence that strengthens type recovery.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PE Yes path PE32 or PE32+ image to scan for MSVC metadata.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --object [] COFF object path. Repeatable. Each object's symbols and sections are correlated against detected metadata. --map (none) MSVC-compatible linker map (.map file). Provides symbol-to-address mappings that anchor metadata locations. --report (none) If provided, writes the full metadata analysis result as JSON to this path in addition to stdout.

docs.title: Return fields (selected)
docs.text: Field Description rtti Recovered RTTI structures: locators, type descriptors, hierarchies. vtables Virtual function tables with their addresses and class associations. unwind Exception handler entries and unwind opcodes. tls TLS callback addresses and data directory info. initializers __xc_a/__xc_z and __xi_a/__xi_z arrays with function pointers.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp metadata-scan target.exe \
    --object obj1.obj --object obj2.obj \
    --map target.map \
    --report metadata.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• inspect-pe — basic PE structure
  • pdb-inspect — PDB type information
  • cpp-recover — recover C++ class models from metadata
  • vtable recover — recover vtables from image and metadata report

docs.title: x86decomp inspect-pe
docs.text: Parses PE32 or PE32+ metadata from a Windows PE binary.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp inspect-pe BINARY

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses the full PE structure: DOS header, NT headers, file header, optional header, section headers, data directories (imports, exports, relocations, resources, TLS, debug, etc.), and section data bounds. Returns the complete PEImage.to_dict() representation. This is a read-only, non-mutating static analysis operation.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description BINARY Yes path PE32 or PE32+ file to parse.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Return fields (selected)
docs.text: Field Description architecture "x86" or "x86_64". file_sha256 SHA-256 of the entire file. machine PE machine type (e.g., 0x014C for x86, 0x8664 for x86-64). image_base Preferred load address. entry_rva Entry point RVA. number_of_sections Section count. size_of_image Total image size in memory. characteristics File characteristics flags. sections Per-section name, virtual address, virtual size, raw size, characteristics. imports Imported DLLs and symbols. exports Exported symbols (if any). relocations Base relocations (if present).

docs.title: Sibling command
docs.text:
The same function is available as the compatibility alias: 

x86decomp project inspect-pe BINARY

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
x86decomp inspect-pe target.exe

{
  "architecture": "x86",
  "file_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
  "machine": 332,
  "image_base": 4194304,
  "entry_rva": 4096,
  "number_of_sections": 4,
  "size_of_image": 655360,
  "characteristics": 271,
  "sections": [
    {"name": ".text", "virtual_address": 4096, "virtual_size": 81920, "raw_size": 81920, "characteristics": 1610612768}
  ],
  "imports": [{"dll": "KERNEL32.dll", "symbols": ["GetProcAddress", "LoadLibraryA"]}]
}

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• pdb-inspect — inspect companion PDB
  • metadata-scan — recover MSVC RTTI/vtable metadata
  • init — uses inspect-pe internally to auto-detect architecture

docs.title: x86decomp pdb-inspect
docs.text: Inspects an MSF 7.0 PDB file and optionally cross-references it against a companion PE.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: x86decomp pdb-inspect PDB [--pe PE_PATH]

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Parses an MSF 7.0 PDB (Program Database) file. The MSF 7.0 format is the debug information container used by modern MSVC toolchains. When --pe is supplied, the parser matches DBI stream source file information, section contributions, and public/global symbol records against the corresponding PE image.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PDB Yes path MSF 7.0 PDB file to parse. --pe No path Companion PE file. When provided, DBI and symbol records are matched against the PE sections and image.

docs.title: Return fields (selected)
docs.text: Field Description pdb_header MSF SuperBlock: block size, free block map, number of directory bytes. dbi Debug information stream: module list, section contributions, source info, stream sizes. dbi.source_info.unique_files Set of source filenames referenced in the DBI stream. public_symbols Public symbol records (name, section, offset) if --pe provided. global_symbols Global symbol records if --pe provided. sections Section contribution records mapped to PE sections if --pe provided. type_info Type information stream summary.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: x86decomp pdb-inspect target.pdb --pe target.exe

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• inspect-pe — parse the PE binary
  • metadata-scan — recover MSVC RTTI, vtables, unwind, TLS metadata
  • target-pack-infer — include PDB evidence in a target pack

docs.title: Artifact Commands
docs.text: Import and verify exported function artifacts with hash-sealed integrity.

docs.title: x86decomp artifact-import
docs.text: Import a validated exported function artifact into a project.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Imports a function artifact directory (containing a function.json manifest, disassembly, decompiled source, and optional Ghidra listing) into a project. The import validates the artifact manifest, copies files into the project's artifact storage, and returns a verification result.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp artifact-import PROJECT EXPORTED_DIR

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root directory EXPORTED_DIR yes path Exported function artifact directory containing function.json

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• EXPORTED_DIR/function.json — validated function manifest with pe-rva:XXXXXXXX identifier and body ranges
  • EXPORTED_DIR/* — disassembly, C source, and optional Ghidra listing files

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • Project artifact storage directory

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp artifact-import ./my-project exported/function_pe-rva-00001234/

docs.title: x86decomp artifact-verify
docs.text: Verify an exported function artifact and its hashes.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Validates a function artifact directory: checks that the function.json manifest is well-formed, that every declared file is present and matches its recorded SHA-256 hash, and that no undeclared files exist. Returns a structured verification result.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp artifact-verify ARTIFACT_DIR

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description ARTIFACT_DIR yes path Function artifact directory to verify

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• ARTIFACT_DIR/function.json — function manifest
  • ARTIFACT_DIR/* — all declared artifact files

docs.title: Verification checks
docs.text: Check Description Manifest validity function.json has valid schema, pe-rva:XXXXXXXX ID, and non-empty body ranges File presence Every file declared in the manifest exists Hash integrity Every file matches its declared SHA-256 hash No extras No undeclared files exist in the artifact directory

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp artifact-verify exported/function_pe-rva-00001234/

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• decompme-pack — Create a decomp.me-compatible packet
  • test-bundle-create — Create a hash-sealed test bundle

docs.title: x86decomp decompme-pack
docs.text: Create a decomp.me-compatible packet from a function artifact.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Builds a local, reviewable function packet from a validated function artifact that is structurally compatible with decomp.me. The packet intentionally does not contact decomp.me. The artifact's Ghidra human-readable listing is retained as source material and labeled non-canonical because decomp.me or compiler syntax may require target-specific conversion.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp decompme-pack ARTIFACT_DIR OUTPUT_DIR [--overwrite]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description ARTIFACT_DIR yes path Function artifact directory containing function.json OUTPUT_DIR yes path Directory to write the decomp.me-compatible packet

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --overwrite false Allow overwriting an existing output directory

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• ARTIFACT_DIR/function.json — function manifest
  • ARTIFACT_DIR/* — disassembly, source, and listing files

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • OUTPUT_DIR/ — decomp.me-compatible packet with manifest, scratch context, and source files

docs.title: Packet contents
docs.text:
File Description metadata.json Packet metadata with function identity scratch.txt Assembler scratch context (binary bytes in hex) target.s or similar Disassembly or decompiled source ghidra-listing.txt Optional Ghidra human-readable listing (labeled non-canonical)

No network contact

This command operates entirely locally. It does not upload data to decomp.me or any external service. Use the decomp.me web interface separately if you choose to share the packet.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp decompme-pack exported/function_pe-rva-00001234/ \
    packets/function_00001234/ \
    --overwrite

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• artifact-import — Import function artifact into project
  • artifact-verify — Verify function artifact integrity

docs.title: Test Bundle Commands
docs.text: Hash-sealed authorized static test bundles for safe artifact exchange.

docs.title: x86decomp test-bundle-create
docs.text: Create a hash-sealed authorized static test bundle.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Creates a ZIP archive containing a x86decomp-test-bundle.json manifest and hash-listed artifact files. The bundle is the preferred way to provide a real regression target without asking the toolkit to execute an uploaded Windows program. All hashes are calculated at creation time and the manifest includes an affirmative authorization statement.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp test-bundle-create OUTPUT --artifact role=path... --authorization TEXT [--name NAME] [--description DESC] [--expected-architecture {x86,x86_64}]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description OUTPUT yes path Output path for the .zip test bundle

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --artifact role=path required (repeatable) Artifact declaration using role=path format; must be specified at least once --authorization TEXT required Affirmative authorization statement (e.g., "I own these artifacts or have permission to analyze them.") --name NAME none Human-readable bundle name --description DESC none Bundle description --expected-architecture {x86,x86_64} none Expected target architecture for validation

docs.title: Supported artifact roles
docs.text: Role Description primary_image Primary PE image (EXE or DLL) to analyze reference_image Reference PE image for comparison candidate_image Candidate (rebuilt) PE image for comparison pdb Matching PDB debug database linker_map MSVC-compatible linker map file coff_object COFF object file from the original build static_library COFF static or import library source Source file compiler_profile JSON compiler profile image_profile JSON image layout profile documentation Supporting documentation

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • OUTPUT — ZIP bundle containing manifest and artifact files

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr

Authorization required

--authorization is a required argument. The manifest records an affirmative statement that you own or are authorized to analyze the artifacts. The inspector rejects bundles without this statement.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp test-bundle-create authorized-target.zip \
    --name "Authorized game build 1.0" \
    --expected-architecture x86 \
    --authorization "I own these artifacts or have permission to analyze them." \
    --artifact primary_image=target.exe \
    --artifact linker_map=target.map \
    --artifact pdb=target.pdb \
    --artifact coff_object=obj/main.obj

For whole-image comparison:

$ x86decomp test-bundle-create image-pair.zip \
    --authorization "Authorized private regression artifacts." \
    --artifact reference_image=original.exe \
    --artifact candidate_image=rebuilt.exe \
    --artifact image_profile=original-image-profile.json

docs.title: x86decomp test-bundle-inspect
docs.text: Safely extract, verify, and statically inspect an authorized test bundle.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Performs static-only operations on a test bundle: verifies archive and artifact hashes, parses PE images, scans bounded MSVC metadata, parses COFF objects and libraries, inspects PDB identity, resolves COMDAT groups, parses linker maps, and compares reference/candidate images when both are supplied. The inspector never executes any binary, object, library, source file, or script from the bundle.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp test-bundle-inspect BUNDLE [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description BUNDLE yes path Test bundle ZIP archive to inspect

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON inspection report to this path

docs.title: Inspection operations
docs.text:
Operation Description Archive verification Validates archive structure and artifact hashes PE parsing Extracts section, import, export, resource, TLS, debug, and relocation metadata MSVC metadata Scans RTTI, vtables, unwind/EH, TLS, and initializer evidence COFF parsing Parses classic COFF and bigobj objects plus bounded static/import archives PDB identity Parses PDB identity, TPI/IPI headers, DBI modules, and contributions COMDAT resolution Resolves COMDAT groups across supplied objects Linker layout Parses MSVC-compatible maps and reconstructs evidenced layout relationships Image comparison Compares reference and candidate images when both are supplied

Static only

The inspector performs only static operations. It never executes any EXE, DLL, object, library, source file, or script from the bundle. Parser success is not a malware-safety determination and is not proof of semantic equivalence.

docs.title: Guardrails
docs.text: The inspector rejects: - Absolute paths, .. components, backslash or drive-qualified paths - Duplicate members, symbolic links - Oversized files or excessive compression ratios - Undeclared or missing artifact files - Artifact hash mismatches - Manifests without an affirmative authorization statement

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp test-bundle-inspect authorized-target.zip \
    --report reports/test-bundle.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• artifact-import — Import function artifact into project
  • artifact-verify — Verify function artifact integrity
  • inspect-pe — Parse PE metadata
  • pdb-inspect — Inspect PDB database

docs.title: x86decomp compile
docs.text: Compile one source file deterministically under a declared compiler profile.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Runs an external compiler as specified by a JSON compiler profile, capturing output hash, tool versions, environment, and timing into a structured compilation report. Designed for matching decompilation workflows where compiler identity and exact argument sets must be reproduced.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp compile PROFILE SOURCE OUTPUT [--report REPORT] [--extra-arg ARG]... [--cache DIR]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROFILE yes path JSON compiler profile (schema version 1 or 2) SOURCE yes path Source file to compile OUTPUT yes path Output file path (directory must exist or be creatable)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON compilation report to this path --extra-arg ARG [] Append an extra argument to the profile's argument list; repeatable --cache DIR none Content-addressed compiler cache directory; avoids redundant builds

docs.title: Profiles
docs.text:
The compiler profile is a JSON document (schema version 1 or 2) with these required fields:

schema_version, id, executable, arguments, timeout_seconds, output_kind

arguments must include the {source} and {output} substitution tokens.

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• PROFILE — compiler profile JSON
  • SOURCE — source file to compile

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• OUTPUT — compiler output (object file, binary, etc.)
  • --report REPORT — structured JSON compilation report

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp compile profiles/msvc-2019-x86-release.json src/candidate.c build/candidate.obj \
    --report build/compile-report.json \
    --extra-arg /O2 --extra-arg /Oi \
    --cache .compiler-cache

docs.title: x86decomp compile-worker
docs.text: Run a bounded isolation compilation worker — local or containerized.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Copies declared inputs into an ephemeral workspace and invokes the compiler profile through a bounded local or container worker. Local mode is not a security boundary; container mode is the production isolation option.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp compile-worker PROFILE SOURCE OUTPUT [--isolation {local_bounded,container}] [--container-image IMAGE] [--cache DIR] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROFILE yes path JSON compiler profile (same format as compile) SOURCE yes path Source file (must be a regular file, not a symlink) OUTPUT yes path Output file path (parent directory created if needed)

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --isolation {local_bounded,container} local_bounded Isolation strategy for the compilation worker --container-image IMAGE none Container image name; required when --isolation container --cache DIR none Content-addressed compiler cache directory --report REPORT none Write structured JSON compilation report to this path

docs.title: Accepted values
docs.text:
• --isolation: local_bounded runs in a temporary workspace on the host; container runs in the specified container image
  • --container-image: any valid container image reference

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• PROFILE — compiler profile JSON
  • SOURCE — source file copied to ephemeral workspace

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• OUTPUT — compiler output, copied from ephemeral workspace
  • --report REPORT — structured JSON compilation report

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp compile-worker profiles/msvc-2019-x86-release.json src/candidate.c build/candidate.obj \
    --isolation container \
    --container-image ghcr.io/example/msvc-2019:latest \
    --cache .compiler-cache \
    --report build/worker-report.json

Local mode is not a security boundary

--isolation local_bounded executes the compiler directly on the host within a temporary directory. Use --isolation container when compiling untrusted source or profiles.

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• compiler-lab — Matrix experiments across multiple profiles and arguments
  • toolchain-register / toolchain-verify — Register and verify compiler executables

docs.title: compiler-lab
docs.text: Run a compiler experiment matrix from a lab manifest.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Compiles one source file under multiple compiler profiles with variant argument combinations, scores each output against a target, and ranks results. Answers: "Which compiler and flags produce the closest match?"

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp compiler-lab LAB [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description LAB yes path JSON lab manifest describing source, profiles, matrix axes, and target

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON lab report to this path

docs.title: Lab manifest schema
docs.text:
The lab manifest is a JSON object with these fields:

Field Required Default Description source yes — Path to source file to compile output_root yes — Directory for per-experiment output subdirectories profiles yes — Array of compiler profile JSON paths matrix yes — Object with axes defining the experiment grid target yes — Object with kind specifying how to score output output_name no — Output filename within each experiment directory cache_root no — Content-addressed compiler cache directory max_experiments no 256 Maximum allowed experiment count; guards against combinatorial explosion

docs.title: Target kinds
docs.text:
pe_function — Compare a COFF symbol against a function body in a PE image:

"target": {
    "kind": "pe_function",
    "pe": "original/game.exe",
    "rva": "0x401000",
    "size": "256",
    "symbol": "_ProcessInput@4"
}

file — Compare a reference file against the compiled output:

"target": {
    "kind": "file",
    "path": "reference/output.obj"
}

docs.title: Matrix axes
docs.text:
Matrix axes values are concatenated as compiler --extra-arg values. The lab computes the Cartesian product of all axis values, then runs each combination against every profile.

total experiments = len(profiles) * len(combinations)

The lab raises an error if the total exceeds max_experiments.

docs.title: Experiment directories
docs.text:
Each experiment creates a subdirectory under output_root:

output_root/
  p000-v0000/
    candidate.obj       compiled output
    compile.json        compilation provenance
    comparison.json     comparison result and score
  p000-v0001/
    ...

docs.title: Scoring
docs.text: Target kind Classification Score pe_function byte_matched 4.0 pe_function relocation_normalized_match 3.0 pe_function instruction_similar 2.0 + similarity bonus pe_function mismatch 0.0 file exact match (equal: true) 2.0 file no match 0.0 – 1.0 (sequence_similarity) any compilation error -1.0

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• LAB — lab manifest JSON
  • Each file referenced by profiles, source, and target

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• Per-experiment output directories under output_root
  • --report REPORT — structured JSON lab report

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr
  • Exit 2 with "compiler lab matrix exceeds max_experiments" if the experiment count exceeds the cap

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp compiler-lab lab-manifest.json --report lab-report.json

With manifest:

{
    "source": "candidate.c",
    "output_root": "lab-output",
    "cache_root": ".compiler-cache",
    "output_name": "candidate.obj",
    "profiles": [
        "profiles/msvc-2019-x86.json",
        "profiles/clang-15-x86-o2.json"
    ],
    "matrix": {
        "axes": {
            "opt": ["/O2", "/O1", "/Od"],
            "arch": ["/arch:IA32", "/arch:SSE2"]
        }
    },
    "target": {
        "kind": "pe_function",
        "pe": "original/game.exe",
        "rva": "0x401000",
        "size": "64",
        "symbol": "_SmallFunc@0"
    },
    "max_experiments": 128
}

This runs 2 profiles x 6 axis combinations = 12 experiments.

Matrix explosion

A matrix with 5 axes of 3 values each x 5 profiles = 1,215 experiments. Set max_experiments to a reasonable cap and review the expected count before running.

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• compile / compile-worker — Single compilation under a single profile
  • diff-bytes — Byte-for-byte comparison of two files
  • diff-function — Compare PE function to COFF symbol

docs.title: x86decomp toolchain-register
docs.text: Register a versioned compiler toolchain in a user-owned registry.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Records compiler executable paths and their SHA-256 hashes in a JSON registry file. The registry is user-owned — proprietary compiler binaries are never copied, only referenced by path. Profiles can then reference toolchain IDs instead of absolute executable paths.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp toolchain-register REGISTRY TOOLCHAIN_ID FAMILY VERSION --executable role=path...

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REGISTRY yes path Path to a JSON toolchain registry file (created if missing) TOOLCHAIN_ID yes string Unique identifier for this toolchain entry FAMILY yes string Compiler family name (e.g. msvc, clang, gcc) VERSION yes string Compiler version string (e.g. 19.29.30137)

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Required Repeatable Description --executable role=path yes (at least one) yes Map a role name (e.g. compiler, linker) to an executable path

Each executable is resolved, verified as an existing regular file, and recorded with its SHA-256 hash and file size.

docs.title: Accepted values
docs.text:
• REGISTRY: any valid filesystem path; the file is created with schema version 1 if it does not exist
  • TOOLCHAIN_ID, FAMILY, VERSION: non-empty strings
  • --executable role=path: role and path separated by =, e.g. compiler=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.38.33130\bin\Hostx64\x86\cl.exe

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REGISTRY — existing registry JSON (if present)

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • REGISTRY — updated registry JSON with the new toolchain entry

docs.title: Registry format
docs.text:
{
    "schema_version": 1,
    "toolchains": {
        "msvc-2022-x86": {
            "id": "msvc-2022-x86",
            "family": "msvc",
            "version": "19.38.33130",
            "registered_at": "2026-07-11T12:00:00Z",
            "ownership": "user_supplied_external_reference",
            "executables": {
                "compiler": {
                    "path": "C:\\msvc\\cl.exe",
                    "sha256": "abc123...",
                    "size": 123456
                }
            },
            "metadata": {}
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp toolchain-register toolchains.json msvc-2022-x86 msvc 19.38.33130 \
    --executable "compiler=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.38.33130\bin\Hostx64\x86\cl.exe" \
    --executable "linker=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.38.33130\bin\Hostx64\x86\link.exe"

docs.title: x86decomp toolchain-verify
docs.text: Verify that all registered toolchain executables are present and their hashes match.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Checks each executable file referenced in a toolchain registry entry. Reports whether each file exists at its recorded path and whether its current SHA-256 matches the registered hash.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp toolchain-verify REGISTRY TOOLCHAIN_ID

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REGISTRY yes path Path to the toolchain registry JSON file TOOLCHAIN_ID yes string Toolchain identifier to verify

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Returns a JSON object:

{
    "toolchain_id": "msvc-2022-x86",
    "valid": true,
    "failures": []
}

If executables are missing or have changed hashes, valid is false and failures lists each failure reason:

{
    "toolchain_id": "msvc-2022-x86",
    "valid": false,
    "failures": [
        "compiler: missing C:\\msvc\\cl.exe",
        "linker: hash changed"
    ]
}

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REGISTRY — toolchain registry JSON

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp toolchain-verify toolchains.json msvc-2022-x86
{"toolchain_id": "msvc-2022-x86", "valid": true, "failures": []}

User-supplied external references

Toolchain executables are never copied or bundled. The registry stores paths and hashes only. You are responsible for maintaining the referenced files at those paths and re-registering after updates.

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• compile / compile-worker — Compile source using a compiler profile
  • compiler-lab — Matrix experiments across multiple toolchains

docs.title: x86decomp convergence-analyze
docs.text: Measure image convergence between a reference and candidate image, with optional history tracking.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Compares a reference PE image against a candidate (rebuilt) PE image using a target-specific layout profile. Classifies observed differences — byte mismatches, section divergences, relocation differences, PE header field differences — and ranks next actions by impact. Optionally compares against a previous report to measure progress and appends the result to a convergence history file.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp convergence-analyze REFERENCE CANDIDATE [--profile PROFILE] [--previous PREV] [--report REPORT] [--history PATH]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REFERENCE yes path Reference (original) PE image CANDIDATE yes path Candidate (rebuilt) PE image to compare

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --profile PROFILE none JSON image layout profile (from image-profile); recommended for accurate section matching --previous PREV none Path to a previous convergence report for delta/progress analysis --report REPORT none Write structured JSON convergence report to this path --history PATH none Append the current result to a convergence history JSONL file

docs.title: Analysis output
docs.text: Field Description reference_sha256 SHA-256 of the reference image candidate_sha256 SHA-256 of the candidate image total_bytes Total image size in bytes matching_bytes Number of bytes that match mismatching_bytes Number of bytes that differ convergence_ratio Ratio of matching bytes to total bytes section_results Per-section comparison with match/mismatch counts remaining_actions Ranked list of next actions for improving convergence previous_comparison Delta from --previous report (if provided) history_entry Appended history line (if --history is specified)

docs.title: Classification
docs.text:
Differences are classified by section kind: - executable — .text and similar code sections - read_only_data — .rdata, .pdata, .xdata - writable_data — .data, .bss - resources — .rsrc - relocations — .reloc

The engine never claims causality unless the mismatch falls inside a declared PE field or normalization range.

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp convergence-analyze original.exe rebuilt.exe \
    --profile profiles/image-profile.json \
    --previous reports/convergence-v1.json \
    --report reports/convergence-v2.json \
    --history convergence-history.jsonl

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• image-profile — Derive a whole-image layout profile
  • image-match — Compare complete PE images
  • release-gate — Evaluate release acceptance contracts

docs.title: x86decomp release-gate
docs.text: Evaluate explicit target release acceptance contracts.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Aggregates exact project-state, evidence, workflow, pipeline, reproducibility, security, and image-convergence records into a release acceptance evaluation. Each contract (workflow, pipeline, reproduction, security, convergence) is evaluated independently. The gate never upgrades a missing report into a pass and never treats the three-source rule as a guarantee of semantic truth.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp release-gate PROJECT [--reproduction-manifest PATH] [--security-report PATH] [--convergence-report PATH] [--require-workflows] [--require-verified-claims] [--require-succeeded-pipelines] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root directory

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --reproduction-manifest PATH none JSON reproducibility manifest (from reproduce-create) --security-report PATH none JSON security audit report (from security-audit) --convergence-report PATH none JSON convergence report (from convergence-analyze) --require-workflows false Require all functions to meet minimum workflow state thresholds --require-verified-claims false Require all claims to have sufficient independent evidence --require-succeeded-pipelines false Require all pipelines to have succeeded --report REPORT none Write structured JSON gate evaluation report to this path

docs.title: Gate evaluation
docs.text: Gate Source Criterion Project state Project check Valid structure, no unrepaired issues Target pack Target pack verification Pack identity and integrity verified Workflow Per-function workflow state Matching ≥ compiles, Functional ≥ differentially_validated (when --require-workflows) Pipeline Pipeline orchestrator All pipelines succeeded (when --require-succeeded-pipelines) Reproducibility Reproduction manifest Manifest verified, all inputs present (when --reproduction-manifest provided) Security Security audit report No blocking findings (when --security-report provided) Convergence Convergence report Convergence ratio meets threshold (when --convergence-report provided) Claims Evidence store All claims have ≥3 independent evidence groups (when --require-verified-claims)

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
The gate returns a structured evaluation with:

  • passed — boolean; true only if every enabled contract is satisfied
  • contracts — per-contract pass/fail with detailed failure reasons
  • summary — human-readable summary of gate status

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success (gate result reported in JSON, passing or failing is data not exit code)
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp release-gate ./my-project \
    --reproduction-manifest reproduce/manifest.json \
    --security-report reports/security-audit.json \
    --convergence-report reports/convergence-v2.json \
    --require-workflows \
    --require-verified-claims \
    --require-succeeded-pipelines \
    --report reports/release-gate.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• reproduce-create — Create reproducibility manifest
  • reproduce-verify — Verify reproducibility manifest
  • security-audit — Security audit
  • convergence-analyze — Image convergence measurement

docs.title: Reproducibility Commands
docs.text: Create and verify reproducibility manifests for declared inputs. A missing external tool is never treated as a successful reproduction.

docs.title: x86decomp reproduce-create
docs.text: Create a reproducibility manifest for declared inputs.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Builds a JSON reproducibility manifest that records the current project state, target pack identity, project structure verification, tool versions, platform metadata, Python environment, and content-addressed storage digests. The manifest declares what is required to reproduce the project's current state. Required external tools can be declared explicitly; their presence and versions are queried but their absence is recorded, not fabricated.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp reproduce-create PROJECT OUTPUT [--required-tool TOOL]...

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root directory OUTPUT yes path Output path for the JSON reproducibility manifest

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --required-tool TOOL [] Declare an external tool required for reproduction; repeatable

docs.title: Manifest contents
docs.text: Section Description project Project verification and state check results target_pack Target pack identity and verification tools Declared and discovered tool versions with availability platform OS, architecture, Python version, and runtime metadata content_store Content-addressed storage digest and integrity verification missing Tools declared as required but not available

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp reproduce-create ./my-project reproduce/manifest.json \
    --required-tool "msvc-2019-x86-cl" \
    --required-tool "ghidra"

docs.title: x86decomp reproduce-verify
docs.text: Verify a reproducibility manifest and all input hashes.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Re-checks every declaration in a reproducibility manifest: verifies the project structure and state, re-validates the target pack, confirms tool availability and version consistency, and checks content-addressed storage integrity. Reports any deviation from the recorded manifest.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp reproduce-verify PROJECT MANIFEST

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root directory MANIFEST yes path JSON reproducibility manifest to verify (from reproduce-create)

docs.title: Files read
docs.text:
• PROJECT — project directory tree
  • MANIFEST — JSON reproducibility manifest

docs.title: Verification checks
docs.text: Check Description Project structure Project verification and state check Target pack Target pack integrity verification Tools Declared tools exist and versions match Content store Content-addressed storage digest integrity Platform Platform metadata consistency

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on any verification failure or missing tool, with structured result

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp reproduce-verify ./my-project reproduce/manifest.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• release-gate — Evaluate release acceptance contracts
  • convergence-analyze — Measure image convergence

docs.title: Ghidra Export
docs.text: Run the bundled Ghidra export workflow to extract function artifacts, symbols, types, and disassembly from a Ghidra project.

docs.title: x86decomp ghidra-export
docs.text:
x86decomp ghidra-export BINARY GHIDRA_PROJECT_DIR GHIDRA_PROJECT_NAME OUTPUT_DIR \
    --scripts-dir ghidra_scripts \
    --ghidra-home PATH \
    --overwrite \
    --selector all \
    --timeout 3600 \
    --report REPORT \
    --print-command

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Execute the bundled Ghidra headless export workflow against a binary imported into a Ghidra project. Exports program metadata, per-function artifacts, and analysis results as structured JSON.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Default Description BINARY yes path — Original binary that was imported into Ghidra GHIDRA_PROJECT_DIR yes path — Ghidra project directory GHIDRA_PROJECT_NAME yes string — Name of the Ghidra project OUTPUT_DIR yes path — Output directory for exported artifacts --scripts-dir no path ghidra_scripts Directory containing the bundled Ghidra export scripts --ghidra-home no path — Path to Ghidra installation (or auto-detected) --overwrite no flag — Overwrite existing export output --selector no string all Function selector filter (all or specific pattern) --timeout no integer 3600 Maximum execution time in seconds --report no path — Write export report as JSON --print-command no flag — Print the constructed Ghidra command without executing

docs.title: Exported Artifacts
docs.text: The export workflow writes two categories of output:

docs.title: Project Manifest (ExportProjectManifest.java)
docs.text: File Content program.json Program metadata and image base sections.json Section headers and RVA ranges functions.jsonl Function list with exact body-range lists symbols.jsonl Symbol names, addresses, and types types.jsonl Recovered type definitions metrics.json Analysis and export metrics

docs.title: Function Artifacts (ExportFunctionArtifacts.java)
docs.text:
For every selected non-external function:

Artifact Content Exact bytes Raw code bytes for every discontiguous range Disassembly Structured instruction and flow records References Cross-references (code, data, calls) Decompiler C Ghidra decompiler output Token tree Clang token/group tree with address bounds P-code raw Raw instruction P-code as JSONL P-code high High-level P-code as JSONL and text Context header Conservative calling-convention context

Ghidra version sensitivity

Ghidra analysis output can change across versions. Run scripts/verify-ghidra.sh against the exact installed release before adopting exports for matching work.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp ghidra-export original/game.exe \
    projects/ghidra GameProject \
    exports/game_export \
    --scripts-dir ghidra_scripts \
    --ghidra-home /opt/ghidra_11.0_PUBLIC \
    --overwrite \
    --timeout 7200 \
    --report export-report.json

Dry-run (print the command without executing):

$ x86decomp ghidra-export original/game.exe projects/ghidra GameProject \
    exports/out --print-command

docs.title: Related Commands
docs.text:
• mcp-tools — list MCP tools in Ghidra
  • mcp-read — invoke read-only Ghidra queries via MCP
  • crosscheck-ghidra — compare toolkit disassembly with Ghidra export
  • artifact-import — import exported function artifacts

docs.title: MCP Commands
docs.text: Commands for interacting with a Ghidra MCP (Model Context Protocol) server: listing tools, invoking read-only queries, proposing mutations, and committing approved changes.

docs.title: MCP Client Selection
docs.text:
All MCP commands require exactly one client transport selector:

Option Description --url URL Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint URL --command-json JSON Stdio MCP client; JSON array of command and arguments

Exactly one required

--url and --command-json are mutually exclusive. Providing both or neither is a fatal error.

docs.title: x86decomp mcp-tools
docs.text:
List tools exposed by a configured MCP endpoint.

x86decomp mcp-tools --url URL
x86decomp mcp-tools --command-json JSON

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description --url one of two string Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint --command-json one of two JSON array Stdio MCP command (e.g., ["python","-m","ghidra_mcp"])

docs.title: Output
docs.text: A JSON array of tool descriptors, each with name, description, and input_schema.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp mcp-tools --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

$ x86decomp mcp-tools --command-json '["python","-m","ghidra_mcp"]'

docs.title: x86decomp mcp-read
docs.text:
Invoke a read-only MCP tool through the project gateway.

x86decomp mcp-read PROJECT TOOL ARGUMENTS --url URL
x86decomp mcp-read PROJECT TOOL ARGUMENTS --command-json JSON

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root TOOL yes string MCP tool name to invoke ARGUMENTS yes JSON object Tool arguments as a JSON object --url one of two string Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint --command-json one of two JSON array Stdio MCP command

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: Read calls are memory-logged in the project. Tool-name heuristics reject probable mutation tool names on the read path. No changes are made to the Ghidra project.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp mcp-read my_project list_functions \
    '{"offset":0,"limit":100}' \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

docs.title: x86decomp mcp-propose
docs.text:
Propose an evidence-linked MCP mutation for approval. Creates a persisted, hash-verified proposal that must be committed via mcp-commit.

x86decomp mcp-propose PROJECT TOOL ARGUMENTS \
    --allow-tool TOOL... \
    --rationale TEXT \
    --evidence EVID... \
    --url URL

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root TOOL yes string MCP tool name to invoke ARGUMENTS yes JSON object Tool arguments as a JSON object --allow-tool yes string (repeatable) Allowlist of permitted tool names --rationale yes string Human-readable rationale for the mutation --evidence yes string (repeatable) Evidence IDs linking the proposal to project evidence --url one of two string Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint --command-json one of two JSON array Stdio MCP command

docs.title: Proposal Workflow
docs.text:
1. mcp-propose describes the intended mutation, rationale, and evidence
  2. The proposal is hashed and persisted in the project
  3. The approval hash is returned for use with mcp-commit
  4. Mutations cannot be executed without the exact approval hash

Mutation guardrail

MCP writes require an explicit allowlist (--allow-tool), evidence, signed proposal, and exact commit hash. This prevents accidental or unauthorized modifications to the Ghidra project.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp mcp-propose my_project rename_function \
    '{"address":"0x401000","name":"MainLoop"}' \
    --allow-tool rename_function \
    --rationale "Symbol confirmed via linker map public symbols" \
    --evidence ev-map-001 ev-crossref-042 \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

docs.title: x86decomp mcp-commit
docs.text:
Commit an approved MCP mutation using the approval hash returned by mcp-propose.

x86decomp mcp-commit PROJECT APPROVAL_HASH \
    --allow-tool TOOL... \
    --url URL

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description PROJECT yes path Project root APPROVAL_HASH yes string Approval hash from mcp-propose --allow-tool yes string (repeatable) Allowlist of permitted tool names --url one of two string Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint --command-json one of two JSON array Stdio MCP command

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Looks up the persisted proposal by approval hash
  2. Verifies the allowlist matches the proposal
  3. Executes the mutation through the MCP gateway
  4. Returns the mutation result

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp mcp-commit my_project abc123def456 \
    --allow-tool rename_function \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

docs.title: Complete MCP Workflow
docs.text:
# 1. Discover available tools
$ x86decomp mcp-tools --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

# 2. Read current state (read-only, no proposal needed)
$ x86decomp mcp-read my_project get_function \
    '{"address":"0x401000"}' \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

# 3. Propose a change
$ x86decomp mcp-propose my_project set_function_name \
    '{"address":"0x401000","name":"MainLoop"}' \
    --allow-tool set_function_name \
    --rationale "MAP symbol at 0x401000 matches MainLoop" \
    --evidence ev-map-001 \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

# 4. Review the proposal (external step by operator)

# 5. Commit the approved change
$ x86decomp mcp-commit my_project <approval_hash> \
    --allow-tool set_function_name \
    --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

docs.title: Related Commands
docs.text:
• ghidra-export — batch export Ghidra analysis artifacts
  • artifact-import — import exported function artifacts
  • evidence-add — register evidence records

docs.title: x86decomp benchmark-run
docs.text: Run a declared benchmark corpus and emit measured results.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Executes every benchmark case declared in a JSON manifest. Each case specifies a kind and compares declared expectations against computed metrics. Benchmark kinds cover byte-level comparison, PE/COFF structural comparison, dynamic differential execution, symbolic bounded comparison, integration scenarios, and classification discovery metrics.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp benchmark-run MANIFEST [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description MANIFEST yes path JSON benchmark manifest declaring cases and expected results

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON benchmark report to this path

docs.title: Benchmark kinds
docs.text: Kind Description byte_pair Byte-for-byte comparison of two files with bounded mismatch reporting pe_coff Compare a linked PE function to its corresponding COFF symbol dynamic Differential execution and comparison of two binaries under a harness symbolic Symbolic bounded comparison of binary functions integration Execute declared integration scenarios with declared observations discovery_metrics Calculate precision, recall, and F1 for function/type discovery against known ground truth

docs.title: Manifest structure
docs.text:
The benchmark manifest is a JSON object with a cases array. Each case declares:

  • kind — one of the six benchmark kinds
  • label — human-readable case identifier
  • target / candidate — paths to files compared (relative to manifest directory)
  • expect — expected outcome assertions (e.g., matched, instruction_similar, differentially_validated)

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp benchmark-run benchmarks/validation-corpus.json \
    --report reports/benchmark-results.json

Example manifest fragment:

{
  "cases": [
    {
      "kind": "byte_pair",
      "label": "function_001_byte_match",
      "target": "obj/msvc2019/function_001.obj",
      "candidate": "obj/gcc11/function_001.obj",
      "expect": { "matched": false }
    },
    {
      "kind": "dynamic",
      "label": "function_042_dynamic_validate",
      "target": "bin/target_function_042.asm.bin",
      "candidate": "bin/candidate_function_042.asm.bin",
      "harness": "harnesses/harness_042.json",
      "expect": { "differentially_validated": true }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• corpus-build — Build the compiler ground-truth corpus
  • corpus-generate — Generate a deterministic source corpus
  • dynamic-validate — Differential binary validation
  • symbolic-validate — Symbolic bounded comparison

docs.title: Corpus Commands
docs.text: Ground-truth corpus creation, building, verification, and comparison. The corpus records source hashes, compiler hashes and versions, complete command lines, environment policy, COFF structure, symbols, COMDAT metadata, and output hashes.

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-create-manifest
docs.text: Create the bundled compiler ground-truth manifest from a repository directory.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Scans a compiler repository for available toolchain executables and emits a reproducible manifest that declares every compiler/version/flag combination to build. The manifest is the input contract for corpus-build.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-create-manifest REPOSITORY OUTPUT

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REPOSITORY yes path Directory containing compiler executables and version metadata OUTPUT yes path Output path for the generated JSON manifest

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REPOSITORY — compiler repository directory tree

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • OUTPUT — JSON ground-truth corpus manifest

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp corpus-create-manifest ./compiler-repo corpus-manifest.json

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-build
docs.text: Build a reproducible compiler/version ground-truth corpus from a manifest.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Reads a corpus manifest and executes every declared compiler/version/flag combination. Each build records source hash, compiler executable hash, version string, full command line, environment, COFF object metadata, and output hash. Results are written as a structured report.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-build MANIFEST OUTPUT_DIR [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description MANIFEST yes path JSON corpus manifest (from corpus-create-manifest) OUTPUT_DIR yes path Directory for compiled outputs and intermediate artifacts

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON corpus report to this path

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • MANIFEST — JSON corpus manifest

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• OUTPUT_DIR — compiled object files and artifacts
  • --report REPORT — structured JSON corpus build report

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp corpus-build corpus-manifest.json build/corpus/ --report reports/corpus-build.json

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-verify
docs.text: Verify source and output hashes in a ground-truth corpus report.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Re-checks every source and output hash recorded in a corpus build report against the current filesystem. Reports mismatches with exact path and expected-vs-observed hash values.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-verify REPORT

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REPORT yes path JSON corpus build report to verify

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REPORT — JSON corpus build report

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp corpus-verify reports/corpus-build.json

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-compare
docs.text: Compare compiler/version ground-truth corpus reports.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Accepts two or more corpus build reports and compares them by compiler version, flag matrix, and per-case output hashes. Reports which cases are byte-identical across corpora and which differ.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-compare REPORTS... [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REPORTS yes path (1+) Two or more corpus build report paths to compare

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Default Description --report REPORT none Write structured JSON comparison report to this path

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REPORTS — two or more JSON corpus build reports

docs.title: Files written
docs.text: • --report REPORT — JSON comparison report

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp corpus-compare reports/msvc-2019.json reports/msvc-2022.json \
    --report reports/corpus-compare.json

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-generate
docs.text: Generate a deterministic parameterized C/C++ source corpus.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Produces a deterministic set of C and C++ source files using a seeded random number generator. Each file exercises specific language features (control flow, integer sizes, struct layouts, calling conventions) to serve as compiler test inputs. The source files and a manifest recording per-file identities are written to the output directory.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-generate OUTPUT_DIR [--cases-per-family N] [--seed SEED] [--c-only] [--cpp-only] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description OUTPUT_DIR yes path Directory for generated source files and manifest

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Default Description --cases-per-family N 8 Number of cases to generate per source family --seed SEED 0x86DEC0DE Deterministic random seed (decimal or hex with 0x prefix) --c-only false Generate only C sources (mutually exclusive with --cpp-only) --cpp-only false Generate only C++ sources (mutually exclusive with --c-only) --report REPORT none Write structured JSON generation report to this path

Mutual exclusion

--c-only and --cpp-only are mutually exclusive. If neither is specified, both C and C++ sources are generated.

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• OUTPUT_DIR — generated .c and/or .cpp source files plus manifest
  • --report REPORT — JSON generation report

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp corpus-generate generated-corpus/ --cases-per-family 16 --seed 0xCAFE \
    --report reports/corpus-generate.json

$ x86decomp corpus-generate generated-corpus/ --c-only --cases-per-family 8

docs.title: x86decomp corpus-generated-verify
docs.text: Verify generated corpus source identities.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Re-checks every source file identity recorded in a corpus generation report against the current filesystem. Verifies that no file has been modified, added, or removed since generation.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp corpus-generated-verify REPORT

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description REPORT yes path JSON corpus generation report (from corpus-generate)

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • REPORT — JSON generation report

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp corpus-generated-verify reports/corpus-generate.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• benchmark-run — Run benchmarks against a corpus
  • corpus-create-manifest — Create the compiler manifest
  • corpus-build — Build the corpus from a manifest

docs.title: DynamoRIO drcov Commands
docs.text: DynamoRIO drcov coverage log parsing and target tracing. The module executes a user-authorized program under drrun -t drcov and parses the text basic-block table.

docs.title: x86decomp drcov-parse
docs.text: Parse a DynamoRIO drcov text coverage log.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Normalizes a drcov -dump_text log file into stable, structured JSON records of modules and basic blocks. Retains the original log path, DRCOV version and flavor, module table entries (base, end, entry, checksum, timestamp, path), and basic-block table entries (module index, start address, size).

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp drcov-parse LOG

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description LOG yes path DynamoRIO drcov text log file (-dump_text output)

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • LOG — drcov text coverage log

docs.title: Output
docs.text: JSON object with source, version, flavor, module_version, modules array, and blocks array.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $ x86decomp drcov-parse traces/drcov.target.exe.0000.proc.log

docs.title: x86decomp drcov-run
docs.text: Run a target executable under DynamoRIO drcov tracing.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Invokes drrun -t drcov with the target executable and declared program arguments, capturing the text coverage log to a temporary directory then copying it to the declared output directory. Supports configurable timeout and the drrun executable path.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp drcov-run EXECUTABLE OUTPUT_DIR [--drrun PATH] [--program-arg ARG]... [--timeout SECONDS] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description EXECUTABLE yes path Target executable to trace under DynamoRIO OUTPUT_DIR yes path Directory for the captured drcov output log

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Default Description --drrun PATH drrun (PATH lookup) Path to the DynamoRIO drrun.exe executable --program-arg ARG [] Argument passed to the target executable; repeatable --timeout SECONDS 300 Maximum time in seconds before the trace is terminated --report REPORT none Write structured JSON trace report to this path

Execution authorization

drcov-run executes the target program under DynamoRIO. Only run executables you own or are authorized to analyze. DynamoRIO instrumentation provides code coverage, not a security sandbox.

docs.title: Files read
docs.text: • EXECUTABLE — target program to trace

docs.title: Files written
docs.text:
• OUTPUT_DIR — captured drcov log file
  • --report REPORT — JSON trace report with metadata and coverage summary

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp drcov-run target.exe traces/coverage/ \
    --drrun "C:\DynamoRIO\bin64\drrun.exe" \
    --program-arg --level --program-arg map1 \
    --timeout 120 \
    --report reports/drcov-run.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• drcov-parse — Parse a drcov coverage log
  • integration-run — Integration scenario execution
  • dynamic-validate — Differential binary validation

docs.title: x86decomp integration-run
docs.text: Execute declared integration scenarios with explicit host execution authorization.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Reads an integration manifest that declares target and candidate executables, input/output observations, and expected exit codes or output hashes. Each scenario runs both the target and candidate processes in temporary working directories and compares only the declared observations. Finite scenarios are never treated as universal equivalence proof.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: x86decomp integration-run MANIFEST [--allow-host-execution] [--report REPORT]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Type Description MANIFEST yes path JSON integration manifest declaring scenarios and observations

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Default Description --allow-host-execution false Explicitly authorize native execution of target and candidate processes on the host --report REPORT none Write structured JSON integration report to this path

Host execution requires explicit authorization

Integration scenarios execute real Windows binaries. The toolkit never runs them without --allow-host-execution. Without this flag the command fails with a contract error. Only enable this flag when you have verified the executables and trust the manifest.

docs.title: Manifest structure
docs.text:
Each scenario declares:

  • name — human-readable scenario label
  • target — path to the reference executable
  • candidate — path to the candidate executable
  • observations — declared outputs to compare (exit code, stdout hash, file outputs)

Path templates {artifact} and {workdir} are resolved against the manifest directory and per-scenario temporary directories.

docs.title: Exit behavior
docs.text:
• Exit 0 on success with JSON result on stdout
  • Exit 2 on argument error with argparse diagnostics on stderr
  • Exit 2 on runtime error with JSON {"error": ..., "message": ...} on stderr
  • Exit 2 if --allow-host-execution is not provided and the manifest requires native execution

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$ x86decomp integration-run integration/scenarios.json \
    --allow-host-execution \
    --report reports/integration-results.json

docs.title: Related commands
docs.text:
• benchmark-run — Run benchmarks including integration scenarios
  • dynamic-validate — Differential binary validation
  • drcov-run — Run target under DynamoRIO tracing

docs.title: x86decomp serve
docs.text: Serve a read-only project API.

docs.title: Purpose
